svg.title: BlueCollar System Architecture
svg.desc: Full system overview: CDN, Next.js App, Express API, Redis, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Stellar Network, Registry Contract, Market Contract, Escrow Contract, Dispute Contract, FeeDistribution Contract, InsurancePool Contract, and labelled data-flow arrows.
svg.text: CDN · Next.js · Express API · Redis · PostgreSQL · Prometheus · Grafana · Loki · Stellar / Soroban
Last updated: 2026-06-25 · Author: BlueCollar Contributors · Update on architectural PRs
Transaction execution · Ledger state · Event stream (Horizon API)
Wallet signing · On-chain verification · TTL-managed persistent storage
Curator → Next.js → REST POST /workers → API → Prisma → PostgreSQL (user/worker records)
API → Registry Contract → Stellar ledger (immutable proof of registration)
Freighter: curator signs tx → Soroban RPC submits → event polled via Horizon → API persists tx hash
Browser → Next.js → GET /workers (paginated) → API → Redis (cache hit?) → PostgreSQL
Optionally: query on-chain via simulateTransaction (read-only, no fee) for verification
User → Next.js → "tip" action → Freighter signs tx → Soroban RPC → Market Contract
Contract: deducts fee_bps → transfers to worker + fee_recipient
Horizon event poll → API updates PostgreSQL job_completion record
Payer creates escrow → funds locked in Market Contract → worker completes work
Release: payer or worker calls release_escrow → funds transferred → event emitted
Dispute: either party files via Dispute Contract → arbitrator resolves → funds released/refunded
Multi-sig: threshold approvals → automatic release; arbitration via request_multisig_arbitration
Maintenance: update this diagram whenever major architectural relationships or contracts change. Source: docs/architecture/system-overview.svg
